---
title: "Assignments"
---

Each week has a short set of readings and an AI tutor assignment. The AI tutor assignment is completed on the [course AI tutor site](#) — link and instructions TBD.

---

## Week 1 (Mar 24–26): The Basics of Economics

**Readings**

- Reading TBD
- Reading TBD

**AI Tutor Assignment**

Complete the Week 1 module on the course AI tutor site by **Sunday, Mar 29 at 11:59pm**.

---

## Week 2 (Mar 31–Apr 2): Causation & Experiments

**Readings**

- Reading TBD
- Reading TBD

**AI Tutor Assignment**

Complete the Week 2 module on the course AI tutor site by **Sunday, Apr 5 at 11:59pm**.

---

## Week 3 (Apr 7–9): Education & Discrimination

**Readings**

- Reading TBD
- Reading TBD

**AI Tutor Assignment**

Complete the Week 3 module on the course AI tutor site by **Sunday, Apr 12 at 11:59pm**.

---

## Week 4 (Apr 14–16): Scaling & Partnerships

**Readings**

- Reading TBD
- Reading TBD

**AI Tutor Assignment**

Complete the Week 4 module on the course AI tutor site by **Sunday, Apr 19 at 11:59pm**.

---

## Week 5 (Apr 21–23): Midterm & Incentives

**This Week**

- Tuesday (Apr 21): **Midterm in class**
- Thursday (Apr 23): Complete the materials below before class.

### Class Prep: Incentives

- [Podcast: Goals, incentives, and choice architecture (Hidden Brain)](https://www.hiddenbrain.org/podcast/success-2-0-getting-what-you-want/)
- [Video: Behavioral economics basics (E4E)](https://econ4everyone.uchicago.edu/video/behavioral-economics)

### Optional Readings

- [Optional Paper: Paying students for performance (NBER)](https://www.nber.org/papers/w15898)
- [Optional Reading: When incentives work, fail, or backfire (JEP)](https://www.aeaweb.org/articles?id=10.1257/jep.25.4.191&lang=en)

### AI Assignment

*No AI tutor assignment this week.*

---

## Week 6 (Apr 28–30): Markets & Equity

### Class Prep: Markets and Their Failures

- [Video: Specialization and efficiency (E4E)](https://econ4everyone.uchicago.edu/video/specialization-and-efficiency)
- [Video: Externalities and market failure (E4E)](https://econ4everyone.uchicago.edu/video/externalities/)
- [Podcast: Research funding spillovers (NPR)](https://www.npr.org/2025/05/28/1253247256/higher-education-federal-funding-universities-science-research)

### Class Prep: Equity vs. Efficiency

- [Video: Equity vs. efficiency, part 1 (E4E)](https://econ4everyone.uchicago.edu/video/equity-vs-efficiency)
- [Video: Equity vs. efficiency, part 2 (E4E)](https://econ4everyone.uchicago.edu/video/equity-vs-efficiency-2/)

### Optional

- [Video: Making a chicken sandwich from scratch (YouTube)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=URvWSsAgtJE)
- [Reading: How food banks use markets (JEP)](https://www.aeaweb.org/articles?id=10.1257/jep.31.4.145)
- Reading: Redistribution and efficiency costs (book)

### AI Assignment

Complete the Week 6 module on the course AI tutor site by **Sunday, May 3 at 11:59pm**.

---

## Week 7 (May 5–7): Uncertainty & Quasi-Experiments

### Class Prep: Statistical Uncertainty

- [Podcast: Polling, prediction, and uncertainty (NPR)](https://www.npr.org/transcripts/463237871)
- [Video: How statistics fool juries (TED)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kLmzxmRcUTo)
- [Article: Why polls can still be wrong (New York Times)](https://www.nytimes.com/2014/07/04/upshot/why-polls-can-sometimes-get-things-so-wrong.html)

### Class Prep: Quasi-Experiments

- [Podcast: Josh Angrist on natural experiments and causal inference (Freakonomics)](https://freakonomics.com/podcast/you-make-me-feel-like-a-natural-experiment/)
- [Article: A nobel prize for natural experiments](https://www.nobelprize.org/uploads/2021/10/popular-economicsciencesprize2021-3.pdf)

### Optional

- [Reading: The credibility revolution in econometrics (JEP)](https://pubs.aeaweb.org/doi/pdfplus/10.1257/jep.24.2.3)
- [Reading: The Card-Krueger minimum wage study (Berkeley)](https://davidcard.berkeley.edu/papers/njmin-aer.pdf)
- [Reading: Technical Nobel background on natural experiments (Nobel Prize)](https://www.nobelprize.org/uploads/2021/10/advanced-economicsciencesprize2021.pdf)

### AI Assignment

Complete the Week 7 module on the course AI tutor site by **Sunday, May 10 at 11:59pm**.

---

## Week 8 (May 12–14): Risk, Insurance & Labor

### Class Prep: Risk and Insurance

- [Video: Adverse selection in insurance markets (E4E)](https://econ4everyone.uchicago.edu/video/adverse-selection/)
- [Podcast: Why insurance matters (Freakonomics)](https://freakonomics.com/podcast/insurance-is-sexy-discuss/)

### Class Prep: Labor Markets

- [Podcast: Labor markets, supply, and demand (Planet Money)](https://www.npr.org/2022/08/10/1116835727/summer-school-5-car-parts-celery-the-labor-market)
- [Podcast: Geography, inequality, and jobs (Planet Money)](https://www.npr.org/sections/money/2014/08/22/342232976/episode-562-a-mall-divided)
- [Podcast: Taxes and work incentives (Planet Money)](https://www.npr.org/sections/money/2013/04/12/177063399/episode-451-why-some-people-love-tax-day)

### Optional

- [Video: Why insurance exists (Marginal Revolution)](https://www.mercatus.org/marginal-revolution-podcast/economics-insurance)
- [Reading: Selection in insurance markets (JEP)](https://www.aeaweb.org/articles?id=10.1257/jep.25.1.115)

### AI Assignment

Complete the Week 8 module on the course AI tutor site by **Sunday, May 17 at 11:59pm**.

---

## Week 9 (May 19–21): Technology, AI & Midterm

**This Week**

- Tuesday (May 19): Complete the materials below before class.
- Thursday (May 21): **Midterm in class**

### Class Prep: Technology, Growth, and AI

- [Video: Productivity and growth (E4E)](https://econ4everyone.uchicago.edu/video/productivity/)
- [Podcast: Restaurant automation and service work (NPR)](https://www.npr.org/2026/03/13/nx-s1-5733110/automation-chef-wok-restaurant-industry)
- [Reading: Why automation does not simply eliminate jobs (JEP)](https://www.aeaweb.org/articles?id=10.1257/jep.29.3.3&page=387)

### Optional

- [Reading: Generative AI and the future of work (Brookings)](https://www.brookings.edu/articles/generative-ai-the-american-worker-and-the-future-of-work/)

### AI Assignment

Complete the Week 8 module on the course AI tutor site by **Sunday, May 24 at 11:59pm**.
